blocks.body: Future Perfect vs Future Perfect Continuous — Future Perfect pokazuje, co będzie już skończone do przyszłego momentu, a Future Perfect Continuous pokazuje, co będzie trwało już jakiś czas do tego momentu.

{"level":"B2","intro":"W tym temacie porównasz Future Perfect i Future Perfect Continuous: czy pytamy co będzie już skończone do tego momentu, czy jak długo czynność będzie już trwała do tego momentu?","rules":[{"label":"FUTURE PERFECT","color":"violet","text":"will have + V3 dla gotowego rezultatu do przyszłej granicy albo deadline'u.","example":"By next week, I'll have finished the report. / By the time you arrive, they'll have left."},{"label":"FUTURE PERFECT CONTINUOUS","color":"rose","text":"will have been + V-ing dla długości trwania albo procesu do przyszłego momentu.","example":"By noon, I'll have been working for two hours. / By next month, she'll have been living here for ten years."},{"label":"KEY QUESTION","color":"blue","text":"Zapytaj siebie: what will already be finished by then? czy how long will it have been happening by then?","example":"completed result / duration up to a future point"}]}

{"title":"1. Szybkie porównanie","intro":"Obie formy patrzą z przyszłej perspektywy, ale stawiają inne pytania:","rows":[{"en":"Future Perfect","ua":"co będzie już skończone do tego momentu","note":"By then, we'll have solved the problem."},{"en":"Future Perfect Continuous","ua":"jak długo proces będzie już trwał do tego momentu","note":"By then, we'll have been working on it for two hours."},{"en":"by then / by next week / by the time","ua":"często prowadzi do gotowego rezultatu","note":"By the time she arrives, I'll have cleaned the flat."},{"en":"for two hours / for ten years / how long","ua":"często prowadzi do długości trwania albo procesu","note":"How long will you have been waiting by then?"},{"en":"result vs duration","ua":"skończony efekt vs proces trwający do granicy","note":"will have finished vs will have been working"}],"warning":"Jeśli najważniejszy jest gotowy rezultat przed deadline'em, wybierz Future Perfect. Jeśli najważniejsza jest długość trwania albo proces do deadline'u, wybierz Future Perfect Continuous."}

{"title":"2. Formy","intro":"Porównaj podstawowe wzory twierdzące, przeczące i pytające.","items":[{"label":"Future Perfect","title":"Twierdzenie","subtitle":"Subject + will have + V3: I will have finished."},{"label":"Future Perfect","title":"Przeczenie","subtitle":"Subject + won't have + V3: I won't have finished."},{"label":"Future Perfect","title":"Pytanie","subtitle":"Will + subject + have + V3? Will you have finished by then?"},{"label":"Future Perfect Continuous","title":"Twierdzenie","subtitle":"Subject + will have been + V-ing: I will have been working."},{"label":"Future Perfect Continuous","title":"Przeczenie","subtitle":"Subject + won't have been + V-ing: I won't have been working."},{"label":"Future Perfect Continuous","title":"Pytanie","subtitle":"Will + subject + have been + V-ing? How long will you have been waiting by then?"}]}

{"title":"3. Kiedy znaczenie się zmienia","sections":[{"label":"Gotowy rezultat do granicy","color":"violet","description":"Wybierz Future Perfect, gdy chcesz podkreślić, że czynność będzie już zakończona do przyszłego momentu.","examples":[{"en":"By next week, I'll have finished the report.","ua":"Do przyszłego tygodnia rezultat będzie już gotowy."},{"en":"By the time you call, she'll have sent the email.","ua":"Jedna czynność skończy się przed innym przyszłym wydarzeniem."}]},{"label":"Długość trwania do granicy","color":"rose","description":"Wybierz Future Perfect Continuous, gdy ważne jest jak długo czynność będzie trwała do przyszłego momentu albo że proces prowadzi do tej granicy.","examples":[{"en":"By noon, I'll have been working for two hours.","ua":"Skupiamy się na długości trwania do południa."},{"en":"By next month, she'll have been teaching here for ten years.","ua":"Skupiamy się na długim okresie do przyszłego punktu."},{"en":"How long will you have been waiting by the time the train arrives?","ua":"Pytanie dotyczy bezpośrednio długości trwania, a nie gotowego rezultatu."}]},{"label":"Równoległy kontrast","color":"blue","description":"Kontekst jest podobny, ale zmienia się fokus: co będzie skończone albo jak długo proces będzie trwał do tej samej granicy.","examples":[{"en":"By Friday, they'll have completed the bridge repairs.","ua":"Pytamy o gotowy rezultat przed deadline'em."},{"en":"By Friday, they'll have been working on the bridge for ten days.","ua":"Pytamy o długość procesu przed tym samym deadline'em."}],"note":"by then / by next week / by the time często prowadzi do Future Perfect, a for two hours / for ten years / how long często prowadzi do Future Perfect Continuous."}]}

{"title":"4. Typowe błędy","items":[{"label":"Mistake 1","color":"violet","title":"Używanie Future Perfect Continuous, gdy potrzebny jest gotowy rezultat","wrong":"❌ By next week, I will have been finishing the report.","right":"✅ By next week, I will have finished the report."},{"label":"Mistake 2","color":"rose","title":"Używanie Future Perfect, gdy pytanie dotyczy długości trwania","wrong":"❌ By noon, she will have studied for two hours.","right":"✅ By noon, she will have been studying for two hours."},{"label":"Mistake 3","color":"blue","title":"Ignorowanie pytania how long?","wrong":"❌ How long will you have finished by then?","right":"✅ How long will you have been working by then?"},{"label":"Mistake 4","color":"amber","title":"Psucie wzoru z been albo V3","wrong":"❌ will have been finished the task / will have finish","right":"✅ will have been working / will have finished"}]}

{"title":"5. Krótki algorytm wyboru","items":["Czy potrzebujesz gotowego rezultatu przed przyszłą granicą? → wybierz Future Perfect.","Czy potrzebujesz długości trwania albo procesu do przyszłej granicy? → wybierz Future Perfect Continuous.","Jeśli masz by then / by next week / by the time i pytanie brzmi „co będzie gotowe?”, często potrzebujesz will have + V3.","Jeśli masz for two hours / for ten years albo pytanie how long?, często potrzebujesz will have been + V-ing.","Jeśli się wahasz, wypowiedz kontrast na głos: gotowy rezultat czy długość trwania do tego momentu?"]}

{"title":"Strony tego działu","items":[{"label":"Will vs Be Going To","url":"/pl/theory/maibutni-formy/will-vs-be-going-to","current":false},{"label":"Future Perfect vs Future Continuous","url":"/pl/theory/maibutni-formy/future-perfect-vs-future-continuous","current":false},{"label":"Future Perfect vs Future Perfect Continuous","url":"/pl/theory/maibutni-formy/future-perfect-vs-future-perfect-continuous","current":true},{"label":"Future Continuous vs Future Perfect Continuous","url":"/pl/theory/maibutni-formy/future-continuous-vs-future-perfect-continuous","current":false},{"label":"Future Forms: Choosing the Right Form","url":"/pl/theory/maibutni-formy/choosing-the-right-future-form","current":false}]}
